# AxioDB — Complete Documentation

Version: 9.6.6 | License: MIT | Node.js >= 20.0.0
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/axiodb
GitHub: https://github.com/nexoral/AxioDB
Docs: https://axiodb.in

---

## What is AxioDB?

AxioDB is an embedded NoSQL database for Node.js with MongoDB-style queries. It is a pure JavaScript alternative to SQLite with zero native dependencies, no compilation, and no platform-specific binaries. It works everywhere Node.js runs — Electron apps, CLI tools, local-first applications, and Node.js backends.

**One-liner:** npm install axiodb and you have a database. No mongod process, no node-gyp, no electron-rebuild.

**Sweet spot:** 10,000–500,000 documents per collection.

---

## Why AxioDB Exists

SQLite requires native C bindings that break in Electron and cross-platform deployments:
- electron-rebuild required on every Electron update
- Platform-specific builds (Windows .node files are not Mac .node files)
- SQL strings instead of JavaScript objects
- Schema migrations required when data model changes
- node-gyp compilation headaches

JSON files (lowdb, plain JSON) have no querying or caching:
- Single file storage means heavy Read/Write I/O
- No built-in caching
- Linear O(n) search for document retrieval
- Performance degrades with larger datasets

NeDB is abandoned and keeps the entire database in memory.

MongoDB needs a separate server process.

AxioDB combines the best of all: embedded like SQLite, NoSQL queries like MongoDB, intelligent caching built-in, pure JavaScript.

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install axiodb
```

Requirements:
- Node.js >= 20.0.0
- npm >= 6.0.0

AxioDB runs on Node.js servers and desktop apps. It does NOT run in the browser.

---

## Quick Start (Hello World in 30 seconds)

```javascript
const { AxioDB } = require('axiodb');

// Create instance (optionally enable built-in GUI at localhost:27018)
const db = new AxioDB({ GUI: true });

// Create database and collection
const myDB = await db.createDB('HelloWorldDB');
const collection = await myDB.createCollection('greetings');

// Insert a document
await collection.insert({ message: 'Hello, Developer!', createdAt: new Date() });

// Query documents
const results = await collection.query({}).exec();
console.log(results[0].message); // Hello, Developer!
```

---

## Core Concepts

### AxioDB Instance

One instance manages all databases and collections. Single instance architecture ensures ACID compliance and data consistency.

```javascript
const { AxioDB } = require('axiodb');

// Basic
const db = new AxioDB();

// With options
const db = new AxioDB({
  GUI: true,           // Enable web GUI at localhost:27018
  RootName: 'MyDB',   // Custom root folder name
  CustomPath: './data', // Custom storage path
  TCP: true            // Enable AxioDBCloud TCP server on port 27019
});
```

### Creating Databases

```javascript
const userDB = await db.createDB('UserDatabase');
const productDB = await db.createDB('ProductDatabase');

// Delete a database
await db.deleteDatabase('OldDatabase');
```

### Creating Collections

```javascript
// Basic collection
const users = await userDB.createCollection('Users');

// Encrypted collection (auto-generated key)
const secure = await userDB.createCollection('SecureData', true);

// Encrypted with custom key
const encrypted = await userDB.createCollection('Payments', true, 'my-secret-key-256bit');

// Schema-validated collection
const validated = await userDB.createCollection('Products', false, undefined, schema);
```

---

## CRUD Operations

### Insert

```javascript
// Insert single document
await users.insert({
  name: 'Alice',
  email: 'alice@example.com',
  age: 30,
  role: 'admin'
});

// Insert many documents
await users.insertMany([
  { name: 'Bob', age: 25 },
  { name: 'Carol', age: 35 },
  { name: 'Dave', age: 28 }
]);
```

### Query (Read)

```javascript
// Get all documents
const all = await users.query({}).exec();

// Filter with operators
const adults = await users.query({ age: { $gt: 18 } }).exec();
const admins = await users.query({ role: 'admin' }).exec();
const found = await users.query({ name: { $regex: /^Ali/ } }).exec();
const inList = await users.query({ age: { $in: [25, 30, 35] } }).exec();

// Chainable methods
const paginated = await users
  .query({ role: 'admin' })
  .Sort({ age: -1 })       // -1 = descending, 1 = ascending
  .Limit(10)
  .Skip(20)
  .setProject({ name: 1, email: 1 })  // Select specific fields
  .exec();

// Find one document
const one = await users.query({ email: 'alice@example.com' }).findOne();

// Count documents
const count = await users.query({ role: 'admin' }).setCount(true).exec();
```

### Query Operators

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| $gt | Greater than | { age: { $gt: 25 } } |
| $lt | Less than | { age: { $lt: 60 } } |
| $gte | Greater than or equal | { age: { $gte: 18 } } |
| $lte | Less than or equal | { price: { $lte: 100 } } |
| $ne | Not equal | { status: { $ne: 'inactive' } } |
| $in | Value in array | { role: { $in: ['admin', 'mod'] } } |
| $nin | Value not in array | { tag: { $nin: ['spam'] } } |
| $exists | Field exists | { email: { $exists: true } } |
| $regex | Regular expression | { name: { $regex: /^John/ } } |
| $or | Logical OR | { $or: [{ age: { $lt: 18 } }, { age: { $gt: 65 } }] } |
| $and | Logical AND | { $and: [{ age: { $gt: 18 } }, { active: true }] } |

### Update

```javascript
// Update matching documents
await users.update({ name: 'Alice' })
  .set({ role: 'superadmin', updatedAt: new Date() })
  .exec();

// Update operators
await users.update({ name: 'Bob' })
  .set({ $inc: { loginCount: 1 } })   // Increment
  .exec();

// Update many
await users.update({ role: 'user' })
  .set({ verified: false })
  .execMany();
```

### Delete

```javascript
// Delete one matching document
await users.delete({ name: 'Dave' }).exec();

// Delete many matching documents
await users.delete({ active: false }).execMany();
```

---

## Aggregation Pipelines

MongoDB-compatible aggregation for data processing:

```javascript
const stats = await users.aggregate([
  { $match: { active: true } },
  { $group: { _id: '$role', count: { $sum: 1 }, avgAge: { $avg: '$age' } } },
  { $sort: { count: -1 } },
  { $project: { role: '$_id', count: 1, avgAge: 1 } },
  { $limit: 10 }
]);
```

Supported aggregation stages: $match, $group, $sort, $project, $limit, $skip, $count

---

## Transactions (ACID)

Full ACID-compliant transaction support with savepoints, rollback, and Write-Ahead Logging:

```javascript
const session = users.startSession({ timeout: 5000 });

// Simple withTransaction (auto-commit/rollback)
await session.withTransaction(async (tx) => {
  await tx.insert({ name: 'Alice', balance: 1000 });
  await tx.update({ name: 'Bob' }, { $inc: { balance: -100 } });
  // Auto-commits on success, auto-rollbacks on error
});

// Manual transaction control
const tx = session.startTransaction();
try {
  await tx.insert({ name: 'Charlie' });
  await tx.savepoint('checkpoint1');
  await tx.update({ name: 'Alice' }, { active: false });
  await tx.commit();
} catch (err) {
  await tx.rollback();
}
```

Transaction methods: insert, update, delete, savepoint, rollbackToSavepoint, commit, rollback

Note: Transactions are scoped to a single collection. Cross-collection transactions are not supported.

---

## Indexing

```javascript
// Create custom index on a field
await users.createIndex('email');
await users.createIndex('age');

// Multi-field index
await users.newIndex('email', 'age', 'role');

// Drop index
await users.dropIndex('email');
```

Auto-indexing: AxioDB automatically indexes documentId for O(1) lookups.
Custom indexes dramatically speed up queries on frequently-filtered fields.

---

## Encryption

```javascript
// Create encrypted collection
const payments = await db.createCollection('Payments', true, process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY);

// Encryption uses AES-256
// Data is stored in .axiodb files with file-level isolation
// Cache entries for encrypted collections are also encrypted
```

Best practices:
- Never hardcode encryption keys — use environment variables
- Use strong, unique keys per collection
- Implement regular key rotation

---

## Built-in Web GUI

```javascript
// Enable GUI at http://localhost:27018
const db = new AxioDB({ GUI: true });
```

GUI features:
- Visual database and collection browser
- Real-time data inspection
- Query execution interface
- Performance monitoring
- No external dependencies required
- Built-in login and role-based access control (RBAC): default `admin`/`admin` account (forced password
  change on first login), predefined Super Admin/Admin/View roles, and a user/role management page for
  Super Admins. Sessions live only in server memory and are lost on restart.

---

## AxioDBCloud — Remote Database Access

Connect to AxioDB from multiple clients over TCP protocol:

### Server Setup (Docker)

```bash
docker run -d \
  --name axiodb-server \
  -p 27018:27018 \
  -p 27019:27019 \
  -e AXIODB_TCP_AUTH=true \
  -v axiodb-data:/app \
  theankansaha/axiodb
```

Ports: 27018 = HTTP GUI, 27019 = TCP remote access. Env vars (all optional, defaults shown):
`AXIODB_GUI=true`, `AXIODB_TCP=true`, `AXIODB_TCP_AUTH=true`, `AXIODB_ROOT_NAME=AxioDB`, `AXIODB_CUSTOM_PATH` (defaults to the container's working directory).
See the Docker Deployment page (https://axiodb.in/docker) for volumes and Docker Compose.

### Server Setup (Node.js)

```javascript
const db = new AxioDB({ GUI: false, RootName: 'MyDB', CustomPath: '.', TCP: true });
```

### TCP Authentication (TCPAuth)

Opt in with `TCPAuth: true` to require a username/password on every TCP connection, reusing the exact same RBAC accounts/roles as the GUI:

```javascript
const db = new AxioDB({ TCP: true, TCPAuth: true, RootName: 'MyDB', CustomPath: '.' });
```

```javascript
const client = new AxioDBCloud("axiodb://localhost:27019", { username: 'admin', password: 'admin' });
await client.connect(); // authenticates automatically
```

Enforcement: every command except PING/DISCONNECT/AUTHENTICATE requires a prior login; the same role permissions as the GUI apply per command; a shared per-IP rate limiter locks an IP out for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts in a trailing 15-minute window (shared with the GUI); accounts still flagged for a forced password change are rejected outright (403) until that's completed via the GUI (no TCP command for it exists yet); an admin-triggered password reset/role change/deletion immediately forces an open TCP connection to re-authenticate. The TCP protocol itself is unencrypted (no TLS) — use a private network, VPN, or your own TLS termination.

### Client Connection

```javascript
const { AxioDBCloud } = require('axiodb');

const client = new AxioDBCloud('axiodb://localhost:27019');
await client.connect();

// Exact same API as embedded AxioDB
const db = await client.createDB('ProductionDB');
const users = await db.createCollection('Users');
await users.insert({ name: 'Alice' });

await client.disconnect();
```

AxioDBCloud features:
- 35+ commands — full CRUD, aggregation, indexing
- Optional authentication (TCPAuth) — shared RBAC with the GUI, per-IP rate limiting
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (up to 10 retries)
- Heartbeat monitoring (PING/PONG every 30 seconds)
- UUID-based request/response correlation
- Connection pooling — supports 1,000+ concurrent connections
- Full TypeScript support
- Zero breaking changes from embedded API

---

## Troubleshooting

- "Not connected to server": `client.connect()` is async and must be awaited before use — it only resolves once the TCP handshake (and, with TCPAuth on, the AUTHENTICATE round-trip) completes.
- 401 "Authentication required": a command was sent before a successful AUTHENTICATE. Pass `{ username, password }` in the AxioDBCloud constructor, or call `await client.login(username, password)` first.
- 403 "This account must change its password before it can be used over TCP": the account is correct but still flagged for a forced password change (true for the default admin/admin, and any newly created user). Complete it via the GUI at http://localhost:27018, then reconnect.
- 429 "Too many failed login attempts": 5 failed logins from one IP within 15 minutes triggers a 15-minute lockout, shared between TCP and the GUI.
- 403 "This is a reserved system database": the `config` database is reserved for AxioDB's own RBAC storage and is blocked on both GUI and TCP, authenticated or not.
- Connection refused/timeout: confirm the server was started with `TCP: true` (or `AXIODB_TCP=true` in Docker, the default) and the port is published/reachable.
- Full guide: https://axiodb.in/troubleshooting — Docker-specific issues: https://axiodb.in/docker and Docker/README.md in the GitHub repository.

---

## Performance Benchmarks

Tested on Ubuntu Linux, Node.js v20+, 10,000 document dataset (March 2026):

**Insert Operations:**
- Single insert: ~3ms
- InsertMany (10 docs): ~87ms
- Schema validation: <1ms

**Read/Query Operations (10K docs):**
- documentId lookup (indexed): ~1ms
- findOne: ~1ms
- Query with $gt: ~469ms
- Query with $in: ~401ms
- Sort: ~382ms
- Full scan: ~2.6s
- Regex scan: ~2.8s

**Update Operations:**
- UpdateOne: ~8ms
- UpdateMany (full collection): ~466ms

**Delete Operations:**
- DeleteOne: ~3ms
- DeleteMany: ~446ms

**Transaction Operations:**
- TX Insert: ~23ms
- TX Update: ~14ms
- TX Delete: ~15ms
- Rollback: ~3ms
- withTransaction: ~10ms

---

## Comparison with Alternatives

### AxioDB vs SQLite / better-sqlite3

| Feature | SQLite / better-sqlite3 | AxioDB |
|---------|------------------------|--------|
| Native dependencies | Yes (C bindings) | No — pure JavaScript |
| Query language | SQL strings | JavaScript objects |
| Schema migrations | Required (ALTER TABLE) | Optional (schema-less) |
| Built-in caching | No | Yes (InMemoryCache) |
| Multi-core processing | No | Yes (Worker Threads) |
| Built-in GUI | No | Yes (localhost:27018) |
| Electron compatibility | Requires electron-rebuild | Works out of the box |
| Best for | 10M+ records, relational | 10K–500K documents, embedded |

### AxioDB vs lowdb / nedb

| Feature | lowdb | nedb | AxioDB |
|---------|-------|------|--------|
| Maintained | Yes | No (abandoned) | Yes |
| Storage | Single JSON file | Single file / in-memory | File-per-document |
| Caching | None | None | InMemoryCache |
| Indexing | None | Basic | Auto + custom |
| Performance at 10K docs | Slow (O(n)) | Medium | Fast (O(1) by ID) |
| ACID Transactions | No | No | Yes |
| Worker Threads | No | No | Yes |
| TypeScript support | Yes | Partial | Yes (full) |

### AxioDB vs MongoDB

| Feature | MongoDB | AxioDB |
|---------|---------|--------|
| Server required | Yes (mongod) | No — embedded |
| Installation | Complex | npm install |
| Network access | Yes | Optional (AxioDBCloud) |
| Scale | Unlimited, distributed | 10K–500K docs, single node |
| Best for | Production multi-user apps | Desktop apps, CLI tools |

**When to use AxioDB:** Desktop apps (Electron, Tauri), CLI tools, local-first applications, rapid prototyping, embedded systems with 10K–500K documents.

**When NOT to use AxioDB:** 1M+ documents, multi-user concurrent web applications, distributed systems, complex relational data with JOINs.

---

## API Reference

### AxioDB (class)

```typescript
new AxioDB(options?: {
  GUI?: boolean;        // Enable web GUI (default: false)
  RootName?: string;   // Root folder name (default: 'AxioDB')
  CustomPath?: string; // Custom storage path
  TCP?: boolean;       // Enable TCP server for AxioDBCloud (default: false)
})

db.createDB(dbName: string, schemaValidation?: boolean): Promise<Database>
db.deleteDatabase(dbName: string): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
```

### Database (class)

```typescript
db.createCollection(
  name: string,
  isEncrypted?: boolean,
  encryptionKey?: string
): Promise<Collection>

db.deleteCollection(name: string): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
db.getCollectionInfo(): Promise<SuccessInterface>
```

### Collection (class)

```typescript
collection.insert(document: object): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
collection.insertMany(documents: object[]): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
collection.query(filter: object): Reader
collection.update(filter: object): Updater
collection.delete(filter: object): Deleter
collection.aggregate(pipeline: object[]): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
collection.findAll(): Promise<any[]>
collection.createIndex(fieldName: string): Promise<SuccessInterface>
collection.dropIndex(indexName: string): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
collection.newIndex(...fields: string[]): Promise<SuccessInterface>
collection.startSession(options?: { timeout?: number }): Session
```

### Reader (chainable)

```typescript
reader.Limit(n: number): Reader
reader.Skip(n: number): Reader
reader.Sort(sort: object): Reader          // { field: 1 } or { field: -1 }
reader.setCount(count: boolean): Reader
reader.setProject(fields: object): Reader
reader.exec(): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
reader.findOne(): Promise<SuccessInterface | ErrorInterface>
```

### Session & Transaction

```typescript
session.withTransaction(callback: (tx: Transaction) => Promise<any>): Promise<any>
session.startTransaction(): Transaction

transaction.insert(document: object): Transaction
transaction.update(filter: object, update: object): Transaction
transaction.delete(filter: object): Transaction
transaction.savepoint(name: string): Transaction
transaction.rollbackToSavepoint(name: string): Transaction
transaction.commit(): Promise<SuccessInterface>
transaction.rollback(): Promise<void>
```

### AxioDBCloud (class)

```typescript
new AxioDBCloud(connectionString: string)  // "axiodb://host:port"
client.connect(): Promise<void>
client.disconnect(): Promise<void>
// All AxioDB, Database, Collection methods available after connect()
```

---

## FAQ

**Q: Is AxioDB a replacement for MongoDB?**
No. AxioDB is embedded — it runs inside your app with no server. MongoDB is a client-server database. Use AxioDB for desktop apps, CLI tools, and local-first apps. Use MongoDB when you need a shared, networked, multi-user database.

**Q: Does AxioDB work with Electron?**
Yes — it was built for this use case. Zero native dependencies means no electron-rebuild, no platform-specific .node files, no node-gyp. Just npm install axiodb.

**Q: What is the difference between AxioDB and better-sqlite3?**
better-sqlite3 uses C native bindings and requires compilation. AxioDB is pure JavaScript — installs without compilation in any Node.js environment. AxioDB also uses NoSQL/JSON documents instead of SQL strings.

**Q: What is the difference between AxioDB and lowdb?**
lowdb stores everything in a single JSON file and gets slow above 1,000 records. AxioDB uses file-per-document tree storage with InMemoryCache, Worker Threads, and auto-indexing — designed for 10K–500K documents with fast queries.

**Q: What is the difference between AxioDB and nedb?**
NeDB is abandoned (no maintenance since 2016). AxioDB is actively maintained, supports TypeScript, ACID transactions, Worker Threads, AES-256 encryption, and AxioDBCloud remote access.

**Q: How many documents can AxioDB handle?**
Optimized for 10,000–500,000 documents. For 1M+ documents, use PostgreSQL or MongoDB.

**Q: Does AxioDB support TypeScript?**
Yes. Full TypeScript type definitions are included out of the box.

**Q: Does AxioDB work in the browser?**
No. AxioDB requires Node.js (v20+) and uses the filesystem. It is server-side / desktop only.

**Q: What is AxioDBCloud?**
AxioDBCloud is a TCP-based client that connects to a remote AxioDB instance. Deploy AxioDB in Docker, connect from multiple clients with the exact same API. Supports 1,000+ concurrent connections with auto-reconnect.

**Q: Is there a web GUI?**
Yes. Enable with `new AxioDB({ GUI: true })`. The GUI runs at http://localhost:27018 and provides a visual database browser, query interface, and performance monitoring. It requires login (default `admin`/`admin`, forced password change on first use) and enforces role-based access control (Super Admin/Admin/View).

**Q: What encryption does AxioDB use?**
AES-256 encryption per collection, with optional custom encryption keys. Store keys in environment variables, never hardcode them.

---

## Limitations

- Dataset size: Optimized for 10K–500K documents. For 10M+, use PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or SQLite.
- Concurrency: Single-instance architecture. Not suitable for hundreds of concurrent web connections.
- Relational data: Document-based NoSQL. No JOIN operations or foreign key constraints.
- Distributed systems: Single-node only. No replication, sharding, or clustering.
- Cross-collection transactions: ACID transactions are scoped to a single collection.

---

## Security Policy

Report vulnerabilities: See SECURITY.md in the GitHub repository.
Do not open public issues for security vulnerabilities.

---
